description: Create a content slide on the expected impact of AI on the risk management workforce, using a two-column layout with a left text callout and a right horizontal bar chart.

header.headline: Expected impact of AI on the size and composition of risk management workforce in next three years
header.sub: As AI tools meet basic analytical needs and handle more routine tasks relative to traditional risks, risk management teams will shift from today’s pyramids to tomorrow’s diamond shapes. Half of European CROs expect to see the shift to a diamond-shaped structure, and 81% of Latin American CROs expect to see a reduction in manual roles.

left.title: What CROs say:
left.body:
AI use cases are being adopted, and long-term efficiency gains will be available, but it requires an uptick in initial headcount to support the build of those long-term capabilities.

AI is going to be a skill to learn, like spreadsheets.

series.label: Stable workforce size with enhanced productivity (e.g., AI boosts efficiency without significantly altering headcount due to redeploying of staff to strategic roles)
Shift to hire specialized expertise as greater needs for specialists in areas like cyber, climate, quantum and AI governance arise (e.g., due to leveraging AI tools to manage more traditional risks)
Shift to a diamond-shaped resource structure vs. traditional pyramid-shaped (e.g., due to use of AI for basic analytical needs)
Significant headcount reduction (e.g., due to AI automating repetitive tasks such as data collection, reporting)
Shift to smaller, specialized tech-savvy teams (e.g., due to AI enabling more efficient operations)
